column1:
User: Someone is threatening me.
Assistant: This may fall under IPC Section 503 - Criminal Intimidation. It deals with threats that cause alarm or fear of harm to person, reputation, or property.

User: I was cheated in a business deal.
Assistant: You may refer to IPC Section 420 - Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property. It applies when someone deceives another to part with property or money.

User: My neighbor is verbally abusing me.
Assistant: This could come under IPC Section 504 - Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of the peace.

User: A person pushed me and tried to hit me.
Assistant: This act falls under IPC Section 352 - Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation.

User: Someone spread false rumors about me.
Assistant: This may fall under IPC Section 499 - Defamation. It punishes harming someone’s reputation through false statements.

User: A man tried to touch a woman inappropriately.
Assistant: This is covered under IPC Section 354 - Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty.

User: My phone was stolen yesterday.
Assistant: IPC Section 379 - Punishment for theft applies here. It punishes theft with imprisonment up to 3 years, or fine, or both.

User: My house was broken into at night.
Assistant: IPC Section 457 - Lurking house-trespass or house-breaking by night to commit offence punishable with imprisonment.

User: Someone forged my signature on a cheque.
Assistant: This comes under IPC Section 465 - Punishment for forgery.

User: I’m getting threatening messages online.
Assistant: IPC Section 507 - Criminal intimidation by anonymous communication can be applied in this case.
